source.title: AMMOS: Automated Molecular Mechanics Optimization tool for in silico Screening

initial_request.research_problem: Virtual screening programs do not easily address structural optimization of compounds, receptor flexibility, and accurate prediction of protein-ligand interactions.
initial_request.target_object: AMMOS tool for automated molecular mechanics optimization
initial_request.known_context: Existing virtual screening tools lack post-docking optimization and receptor flexibility, leading to inaccurate binding pose prediction and scoring.
constraints: Must allow partial to full atom flexibility
input_data: Small molecules: raloxifene, FDI, thymidine, PR2
Chemical library of 37970 single conformer molecules
quantifiable_goals: Improve enrichment of active compounds in top 3-5% of database
Retrieve 40-60% of initially added active compounds after minimization

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Virtual screening programs lack structural optimization and receptor flexibility, leading to inaccurate predictions.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether post-docking optimization can improve enrichment in structure-based virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Post-docking optimization and structural refinement of compounds can improve the efficiency of structure-based virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop an automated tool for post-docking minimization of protein-ligand complexes.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Previous studies suggest that post-docking energy minimization can significantly improve enrichment for some protein targets.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design and implement AMMOS to perform automatic minimization.

evidence.quote_or_span: available computer programs do not easily address problems such as: structural optimization of compounds in a screening library, receptor flexibility/induced-fit, and accurate prediction of protein-ligand interactions

It has been shown that structural optimization of chemical compounds and that post-docking optimization in multi-step structure-based virtual screening approaches help to further improve the overall efficiency of the methods.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that post-docking optimization improves enrichment.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for an automated tool to refine 3D structures and energy minimize protein-ligand complexes.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AMMOS using AMMP can automatically refine small molecules and protein-ligand complexes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop AMMOS with AMMP for fast energy minimization, supporting partial to full flexibility.
evolution_trajectory.observation: AMMOS was developed to automatically refine 3D structures and energy minimize complexes.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test minimization protocols on small molecules to select optimal settings.

evidence.quote_or_span: we developed the program AMMOS for refining both, the 3D structures of the small molecules present in chemical libraries and the predicted receptor-ligand complexes through allowing partial to full atom flexibility through molecular mechanics optimization.
AMMOS proposes relatively fast energy minimization by making use of the full-featured molecular mechanics program AMMP

AMMP has several advantages that make it relevant for the present applications such as: a fast multipole method for including all atoms in the calculation of long-range potentials and robust structural optimizers.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: AMMOS designed but minimization protocol not yet selected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which optimization method and force field yield best minimization for small molecules.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Testing multiple methods and force fields will identify the optimal protocol.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test five optimization methods (steepest descent, conjugate gradient, BFGS, simplex, genetic algorithm) with sp4 and sp5 force fields on four small molecules.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Conjugate gradient and GA produced lowest energies; GA was slower. Steepest descent and simplex gave higher energies.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze results to select default protocol.

evidence.quote_or_span: Table 1 and Additional file 1 present the results for the four molecules and the five different applied optimization methods.
conjugate gradient and GA produced lowest energies, however GA was much slower due to its stochastic nature.
For all tested molecules, steepest descent and simplex search led to minimized structures with higher energies.
optimized, while conjugate gradient succeeded to overcome the clashes (results not shown).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Minimization results obtained for different methods and force fields.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which force field performs better for energy minimization.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: sp5, being an improved version, should minimize better than sp4.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare sp4 and sp5 energy differences.
evolution_trajectory.observation: sp4 minimized slightly better than sp5, contrary to expectation, especially for thymidine and raloxifene.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select conjugate gradient with sp4 as default protocol.

evidence.quote_or_span: Although sp5 has been reported to be an improved version of sp4 [39], in our work sp4 minimized slightly better than sp5 in terms of energy differences between the initial and minimized structure, in particular for thymidine and raloxifene.
sp4 minimized slightly better than sp5 in terms of energy differences between the initial and minimized structure, in particular for thymidine and raloxifene.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Conjugate gradient and sp4 identified as best performing.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to finalize minimization protocol parameters.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Conjugate gradient with sp4, 2×500 iterations, and nonbon potential will be effective.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Set default protocol: conjugate gradient, sp4 force field, 2×500 iterations, nonbon potential.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Protocol selected based on performance and robustness.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate protocol against other force fields.

evidence.quote_or_span: Following the above observations, we chose conjugate gradient and sp4 as the minimization protocol for subsequent assessment of AMMOS.
we chose conjugate gradient and sp4 as the minimization protocol for subsequent assessment of AMMOS.
we accepted 2×500 iterations as an appropriate number of steps for the automatic AMMOS procedure
we selected the nonbon potential for further evaluation of AMMOS

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether AMMOS minimization results are comparable to established force fields.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AMMOS results will be close to those from MMFF94s and Tff force fields.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare AMMOS minimized structures with those from MMFF94s and Tff.
evolution_trajectory.observation: AMMOS results are close to those obtained by MMFF94s and Tff force fields.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply AMMOS to large chemical library and protein-ligand complexes.

evidence.quote_or_span: the results obtained after minimization with AMMOS are close to those obtained by other force fields.
The performance of our package was evaluated by comparing the structures of small chemical entities minimized by AMMOS with those minimized with the Tripos and MMFF94s force fields.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Performance on large chemical library and protein-ligand complexes.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AMMOS can efficiently minimize large libraries and improve enrichment in virtual screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Apply AMMOS to 37970 molecules and to protein-ligand complexes from multi-step virtual screening on ER and NA.

parameters.complexes: pre-docked containing 60% of initially added inhibitors

evolution_trajectory.observation: 76% of molecules had ΔE below 50 kcal/mol; enrichment assessed for protein-ligand complexes.

evidence.quote_or_span: The AMMOS procedure for minimization of small compounds was applied to a chemical library of 37970 single conformer molecules

AMMOS was used for full flexible minimization of protein-ligands complexes obtained from a mutli-step virtual screening. Enrichment studies of the selected pre-docked complexes containing 60% of the initially added inhibitors were carried out with or without final AMMOS minimization on two protein targets having different binding pocket properties.

de 1kim); thieno [3,2-b]pyridine-2-sulfonic acid [2-oxo-1-(1h-pyrrolo[2,3-c]pyridin-2-ylmethyl)-pyrrolidin-3-yl]-amide (PR2) (an inhibitor of coagulation factor X, PDB code 1f0r). To test the performance of AMMOS on a large number of small organic molecules we used the ChemBridge diversity set $[55]$ . Our decoy library contained 37970 molecules after ADME/Tox filtering with the program Filter 1.0.2 [43]. We merged 20 known active compounds for ER and NA (with activities ranging from micromolar to nanomolar) and

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether AMMOS minimization improves enrichment for ER and NA.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AMMOS will improve enrichment, especially with partial flexibility for ER and rigid receptor for NA.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare enrichment with and without AMMOS minimization.
evolution_trajectory.observation: AMMOS improved enrichment for ER with partial flexibility (cases 3,4) and for NA with rigid receptor (case 5).

evidence.quote_or_span: when the protein was kept rigid (case 5, green), slightly better results for NA were achieved after AMMOS minimization.
AMMOS performs well and its use can help to improve enrichment in multi-step SBVLS protocols.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Quantitative enrichment in top percentages of database.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: AMMOS rescoring will place a high percentage of actives in top 3-5%.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Calculate percentage of actives in top 5% after AMMOS rescoring.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 40% of NA inhibitors and 60% of ER actives found in top 5% of compound libraries.

evidence.quote_or_span: AMMOS was able to improve the enrichment after the pre-docking stage with 40 to 60% of the initially added active compounds found in the top 3% to 5% of the entire compound collection.
after AMMOS rescoring, we found 40% of the NA inhibitors and 60% of the ER active compounds in the top 5% of the compound libraries (2000 compounds).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Post-docking minimization may worsen enrichment for some proteins.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Large pockets with small ligands may lead to worse enrichment after minimization.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze cases where enrichment did not improve (NA, thymidine kinase).
evolution_trajectory.observation: For NA and thymidine kinase, ligands do not fill binding sites well (volume ratio 0.44 and 0.45), leading to worsened enrichment.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitation and plan future improvements.

evidence.quote_or_span: we did not obtain better enrichment after AMMOS in the case of NA
for some binding pockets and ligands, allowing post-docking relaxation of the protein-ligand complexes may worsen the enrichment
For NA and thymidine kinase, the ligands do not fill well the binding sites (with a Volume of the ligand/Volume of the pocket ratio of 0.44 and 0.45, respectively).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: AMMOS does not consider solvation effects.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Including solvation could further improve accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitation and plan future implementation of solvation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Current AMMOS version lacks solvation; automated protocol planned for future release.

evidence.quote_or_span: The present version of AMMOS does not take into consideration possible solvation effects
Automated protocol will be implemented in a future release of AMMOS to treat solvation and water molecules.

validation_methods: Comparison with MMFF94s and Tff force fields
Enrichment studies on ER and NA targets
metrics: Percentage of actives in top 3-5% of database
success_verification.final_conclusion: AMMOS improves enrichment in multi-step SBVLS, retrieving 40-60% of actives in top 3-5%, but may worsen enrichment for targets with large pockets and small ligands; solvation effects not considered.
limitations: Post-docking minimization can worsen enrichment for some proteins (e.g., NA, thymidine kinase) due to large pockets with small ligands.
AMMOS does not consider solvation effects; future release planned.
